message:
refactor(ruby): Major v2.0 release with restructured code organization

Upgrade Ruby module from v1.5 to v2.0 with significant code reorganization,
improved error handling, and enhanced rule filtering capabilities.

Code structure reorganization:
- Moved color/symbol initialization after globals declaration for logical flow
- Relocated error handling (on_err, trap) after color definitions so error
  messages can safely reference color variables
- Conditional color assignment: Only define when USE_COLOR=1, otherwise
  use null coalescing pattern (: "${RED:=}") for safety
- Better section ordering: Globals → Colors → Error handling → CLI parsing

CLI enhancements:
- --only-rules=GLOB: Filter to specific ast-grep rules by glob pattern
  (e.g., --only-rules="ruby.security.*")
- --disable-rules=GLOB: Exclude specific rules from scan
  (e.g., --disable-rules="ruby.style.*")
- --very-verbose: Enhanced verbosity beyond normal --verbose mode
- Better flag documentation in help text

Error handling improvements:
- JSON/SARIF-aware on_err(): Emit structured error JSON when in those formats
  instead of colorized text messages
- Escape double quotes in error cmd string for valid JSON
- Format detection: Check ${FORMAT:-text} to determine output mode
- Safer color variable expansion: Only use colors after they're defined

Metadata preservation:
- ASYNC_ERROR_* arrays removed from top of file (moved/consolidated)
- Clean metadata organization for future rule categories
- Maintained compatibility with existing rule IDs and severity levels

Code quality:
- Removed redundant whitespace and formatting inconsistencies
- Better variable initialization patterns
- Consistent use of [[ ]] test syntax
- Safer color variable defaults with null coalescing

Rule filtering architecture:
- ONLY_RULES and DISABLE_RULES support glob patterns
- Integrates with ast-grep --pattern flag for selective execution
- Enables focused scans in large codebases (e.g., only security rules)
- Supports CI optimization by disabling style/format checks

Backward compatibility:
- All existing flags and behaviors preserved
- Output formats unchanged
- Rule IDs and categories maintained
- Default behavior identical to v1.5

This restructuring improves code maintainability and sets the stage for
additional Ruby-specific analysis rules and external tool integrations
(RuboCop, Brakeman, bundler-audit) in future releases.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
